text.title: Process Mining & Automation Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real applications of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Discovery output shows only the most frequent variant; long-tail variance is hidden.
antipattern.root-cause: Author filters out 'noise' variants without checking cost.
antipattern.fix: Keep ≥top-10 variants by frequency × cost product; never top-1 only.

antipattern.symptom: Candidates are listed without feasibility scores or owners.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats discovery as the deliverable.
antipattern.fix: Require feasibility enum + named owner per candidate.

antipattern.symptom: Event log window is older than 3 months.
antipattern.root-cause: Author works from a one-time CSV dump.
antipattern.fix: Require data_window.to within 90 days; refuse stale exports.
